blurb: Ensures all eligible Albertans have health care insurance coverage, administers drug benefit and aids to daily living programs, supports access to primary care and family doctors, delivers immunization and communicable disease prevention programs, and regulates health professions.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans have access to health care coverage and benefits
goals.description: The ministry ensures all eligible Albertans have health care insurance coverage and access to supplementary health benefits including drug coverage, aids to daily living, and programs for children, adults, and seniors.

foundational_services.name: Administer health insurance policy and benefits program design
foundational_services.desc: Develops policy for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan, sets benefit schedules and physician fee schedules, negotiates with health professional associations, and administers the overall insurance framework that funds medically necessary services.
foundational_services.users: Government policy staff, health professional associations

main_services.name: Register for and manage your health care insurance

supporting.name: Register for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan
supporting.desc: New Alberta residents, newborns, and returning Canadians can register for AHCIP to receive publicly funded health care coverage. Registration provides a personal health number and Alberta health care card.
supporting.users: New Alberta residents, parents of newborns, returning Canadians

reach_estimate.basis: All eligible Alberta residents are enrolled in AHCIP

economic_impact.note: Foundational public health insurance providing access to medically necessary services for all Albertans

relationships.service: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta

supporting.name: Update your health care insurance information or replace your card
supporting.desc: Albertans can update their address, name, or other personal information on their AHCIP registration, and request replacement Alberta health care cards when lost or damaged.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of updates annually
reach_estimate.basis: Albertans frequently move, change names, or lose cards

economic_impact.note: Ensures continuous access to insured services by keeping records current

relationships.service: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta

supporting.name: Check your health coverage status or claim history
supporting.desc: Albertans and health care providers can verify coverage status, check claim history, and confirm eligibility for insured services through online portals and phone inquiries.
supporting.users: Insured Albertans, health care providers, billing staff

reach_estimate.basis: Every insured visit triggers a coverage check

economic_impact.note: Supports efficient billing and prevents service denials due to registration gaps

main_services.name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage

supporting.name: Apply for coverage under a government drug benefit program
supporting.desc: Albertans who qualify can apply for drug coverage through programs such as Non-Group Coverage, Coverage for Seniors, Alberta Child Health Benefit, or Alberta Adult Health Benefit. These programs cover prescription drugs listed on the Alberta Drug Benefit List.
supporting.users: Seniors, low-income adults, families with children, individuals without employer drug plans

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 900,000 Albertans covered by government drug programs
reach_estimate.basis: Includes seniors, low-income, and uninsured Albertans

economic_impact.note: Provides affordable access to prescription drugs for vulnerable populations

supporting.name: Look up whether a drug is covered on the Alberta Drug Benefit List
supporting.desc: The Alberta Drug Benefit List is a searchable directory of prescription drugs and their coverage status under government drug benefit programs. Pharmacists, physicians, and patients use it to determine coverage eligibility.
supporting.users: Pharmacists, physicians, patients enrolled in drug programs

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of lookups daily by providers and patients
reach_estimate.basis: Primary reference for drug coverage decisions across the province

economic_impact.note: Informs prescribing and dispensing decisions to ensure patients receive covered medications

supporting.name: Apply for aids to daily living benefits
supporting.desc: The Alberta Aids to Daily Living program helps Albertans with a chronic disability or illness by providing funding for basic medical equipment and supplies such as wheelchairs, respiratory equipment, prosthetics, and ostomy supplies.
supporting.users: Albertans with chronic disabilities, home care clients, health providers

reach_estimate.basis: Long-standing program serving Albertans with chronic conditions

economic_impact.note: Enables independent living and reduces hospital and care facility admissions

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans can access primary and preventive health care
goals.description: The ministry supports access to primary care providers, administers public health programs including immunization and communicable disease prevention, and connects Albertans with health information and advice.

foundational_services.name: Plan and fund primary care delivery and health workforce
foundational_services.desc: Develops funding models and delivery frameworks for primary care including physician compensation, primary care network governance, and health workforce planning to ensure Albertans have access to family doctors and primary care teams.
foundational_services.users: Government, primary care networks, regional health authorities

main_services.name: Find primary care and get health advice

supporting.name: Find a family doctor or primary care provider
supporting.desc: Albertans looking for a family doctor can search for physicians accepting new patients and learn about primary care options in their community, including Primary Care Networks and walk-in clinics.
supporting.users: Albertans without a family doctor, new residents

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 800,000 Albertans reported not having a family doctor
reach_estimate.basis: A significant proportion of the population seeks primary care access

economic_impact.note: Primary care access reduces emergency department visits and improves population health outcomes

supporting.name: Get advice on a health concern
supporting.desc: Health Link 811 is a 24/7 telephone service staffed by registered nurses providing health advice, symptom assessment, and direction to appropriate health services. Available to all Albertans.

reach_estimate.basis: Primary health advice line for the province's population

economic_impact.note: Reduces unnecessary emergency visits by providing accessible nurse-led health advice

supporting.name: Access your personal health records online
supporting.desc: MyHealth Records allows Albertans to view their immunization history, lab results, medication dispensing history, and hospital visit records through a secure online portal.

reach_estimate.basis: Growing adoption of personal health record access

economic_impact.note: Empowers patients with their own health information and reduces duplicate testing

main_services.name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases

supporting.name: Get routine or travel immunizations
supporting.desc: Alberta provides publicly funded routine immunizations for children and adults through public health offices, pharmacies, and physician offices. Travel immunizations are also available. The immunization schedule covers diseases from infancy through adulthood.

channels.label: Public health offices, pharmacies, physician offices

reach_estimate.basis: Routine childhood and adult immunization programs province-wide

economic_impact.note: Prevents outbreaks and reduces long-term health system costs through disease prevention

supporting.name: Report or learn about a notifiable disease
supporting.desc: Health care providers are required to report notifiable communicable diseases to the Chief Medical Officer of Health. The ministry publishes surveillance reports and provides guidance on disease prevention and outbreak management.
supporting.users: Health care providers, public, outbreak response teams

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of reports annually
reach_estimate.basis: Mandatory reporting by all health providers for listed diseases

economic_impact.note: Early detection and response prevents disease spread and protects population health

supporting.name: Access health promotion and disease prevention resources
supporting.desc: The ministry provides evidence-based resources on healthy living, chronic disease prevention, smoking cessation, cannabis health effects, and other public health topics to help Albertans make informed health choices.
supporting.users: All Albertans, health educators, community organizations

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of page views and resource downloads
reach_estimate.basis: Broad public health education mandate reaching diverse audiences

economic_impact.note: Preventive health information reduces chronic disease burden and long-term health costs

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Health professions are regulated and accountable
goals.description: The ministry oversees the regulatory framework for over 30 health professions through self-governing colleges, ensuring practitioners meet registration, competence, and conduct standards that protect the public.

foundational_services.name: Develop health workforce policy and professional regulation standards
foundational_services.desc: Sets policy for health professions regulation, oversees self-governing regulatory colleges, and develops standards for registration, continuing competence, and professional conduct across 30+ regulated health professions.

main_services.name: Register or verify a regulated health professional

supporting.name: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta
supporting.desc: Health professionals regulated under the Health Professions Act must register with their respective regulatory college. The ministry oversees the regulatory framework governing over 30 regulated health professions and their colleges.
supporting.users: Physicians, nurses, pharmacists, dentists, and other regulated health professionals

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 100,000 registered health professionals in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Ensures only qualified professionals deliver health services, protecting patient safety

relationships.service: Register for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan
Update your health care insurance information or replace your card

supporting.name: Verify a health professional's registration status
supporting.desc: Albertans can check whether a health professional is registered and in good standing with their regulatory college. Each college maintains a public register of members.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of public register searches monthly
reach_estimate.basis: Public verification of health professional credentials

economic_impact.note: Public transparency protects patients and supports employer due diligence

supporting.name: File a complaint about a regulated health professional
supporting.desc: Albertans can file complaints about the conduct, competence, or capacity of a regulated health professional with their respective regulatory college. The college investigates and may take disciplinary action.
supporting.users: Patients, family members, other health providers

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of complaints filed annually across all colleges

economic_impact.note: Accountability mechanism that maintains public trust in health professions
